title: Two Truths and a Lie
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: Each person says two truths and one lie. The group tries to identify which statement is the lie.
online_adaptation_notes: Ask each participant to post two truths and one lie. Others react with emoji or reply with the number they think is false.
facilitation_notes: Keep examples light and avoid asking for sensitive personal information.

title: Animal/Adjective Game
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: Everyone chooses an animal or adjective that starts with the same letter as their first name, such as Massive Mark or Tiny Tina.
online_adaptation_notes: Ask everyone to post a nickname made from an adjective or animal plus their name. Optionally ask for one sentence about why they picked it.
facilitation_notes: Make it optional to explain the choice so the activity stays low-risk.

title: My Good Neighbor
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: A person in the middle reveals something true about themselves. Anyone it applies to finds a different seat.
online_adaptation_notes: One person posts a light statement starting with 'My good neighbor is anyone who...'. Others reply if it applies to them.
facilitation_notes: Use low-stakes prompts such as food, hobbies, commute, or class routines.

title: Telephone Charades
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: Players pass along acted-out clues and the final person guesses the movie or phrase.
online_adaptation_notes: Use emoji-only clues or three-word clues in a thread. Each person adds a clue, and the final person guesses.
facilitation_notes: Avoid making people record video in low-comfort groups.

title: Bowl of Jello
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: The facilitator calls a prompt and participants perform the matching action before a countdown ends.
online_adaptation_notes: Turn actions into emoji reactions. The facilitator posts a prompt and participants quickly reply with the matching emoji set.
facilitation_notes: Use it only when the room already tolerates playful prompts.

title: Mingle Mingle
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: People mingle until the facilitator calls a number, then they quickly form groups of that size.
online_adaptation_notes: Use a poll or prompt. People with the same answer join a short thread and exchange one quick response.
facilitation_notes: This is higher-risk online because it asks people to break into visible subgroups.

title: Blanket Game
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: Two teams sit on opposite sides of a blanket. When it drops, the first person to say the other's name wins.
online_adaptation_notes: Post small clues about a participant and let the group guess who it is. Keep clues voluntary and non-sensitive.
facilitation_notes: Works best after participants have introduced themselves at least once.

title: Zip Zip Bong
source: UCSD Sixth College Community Building Activities
instructions: Players pass silly sounds around the circle while trying not to laugh.
online_adaptation_notes: Run a quick chain where each participant replies with either 'zip zip' or 'bong' and tags the next person.
facilitation_notes: Avoid in very quiet rooms because it can feel performative.
